name: yaw_long_hold
description: Single-direction full yaw held much longer than yaw_reversal.json's 0.5s segment or yaw_spool_up.json's 0.6s, to directly test whether the reversal maneuver's short hold reaches the same steady-state rate a longer hold does (raised by harald: shipTypes.ts's 52 deg/s yaw spec was itself measured via a multi-second sustained rotation, not a short burst -- if the real settling time constant is longer than modeled, a short segment may plateau below the true sustained rate). Sized to stay just inside frame for continuous pixel tracking at FOV 116 (58 deg half-FOV): using this project's own fitted tau~0.19s and steady-state~47.5 deg/s, the integrated sweep only reaches ~38 deg by t=1.0s and ~53 deg by t=1.3s, safely under the 58 deg half-FOV limit -- do not extend past ~1.3s without re-checking against the actual FOV in use, or the landmark will exit frame and lost-lock will trigger (see analysis output for the peak-brightness warning).
